data: Two large, joining fragments of a marble architrave (or similar). Thick slabs of a rectangular form, of a fine white marble with occasional blue veins. The lower edge is square in profile, the face is very lightly concave, while the top edge is rounded with a moulding on the rear side (relative to the inscribed face) of the upper edge. The rear face is straight (i.e. not convex), and only very roughly finished, indicating that additional blocks were set against it. Both fragments are broken at left and right, intact on the other sides.The implication seems to be that the inscribed face was horizontal and faced upwards, with the moulded edge facing outwards, and a structure underneath supporting the blocks. Fragment A (left) is 67.7 cm wide, fragment B 68.1 cm wide.

Fine-grained, homogeneous, white, calcitic marble, likely Lunense. Optical microscopy: homeoblastic fabric with triple points, mosaic microstructure. Digital microscopy: homeoblastic fabric (identification based on pXRF, digital microscopy, XRD, optical microscopy, stable isotopes analysis, EPR, LA-ICP-MS)
A single line of monumental letters set neatly in the vertical middle of the available field.

The letters are deeply square cut (7-12 mm deep, typically 8-10), without serifs, for the insertion of bronze letters, with triangular interpuncts and use of supraline on numeral. Bronze is preserved in the three interpuncts in fragment A, and the letter N in fragment A preserves some of the fixing material for the original bronze letter. Note that the I of 'vir' is extended, 138mm tall.

Found in the context of excavation of a structure in the area between the castle and the duomo (precise description of the location varies, CIL says 'in piazza del Duomo', Salinas in NSA refers to excavations in the vicinity of the castle rock).
Prag 2022-07-13, in the courtyard of the Museo Civico.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana; photo J.Prag 2022-07-13

A large and well-displayed collection. It begins with three ante-rooms, in the first of which is a rich display of lithics (mainly Neolithic) from the surrounding area. A display of material from Himera (lion-head spouts from the Temple of Victory and red-figure craters being the principle pieces) and a numismatic gallery then follow before the main room which contains Hellenistic and Roman material from Termini Imerese itself. Small finds, sculpture and inscriptions predominate. The museum collection continues upstairs with non-archaeological material (and a single inscription). The courtyard of the building holds a large collection of architectural pieces and inscriptions
Via Marco Tullio Cicerone, 90018 - Termini Imerese (PA)

Accademia nazionale dei Lincei, Istituto nazionale di archeologia e storia dell’arte (Italy), e Reale Accademia d’Italia, «Notizie degli scavi di antichità», Notizie degli Scavi di Antichità, 1876, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/1646037
Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae

T. Mommsen, Inscriptiones Bruttiorum Lucaniae Campaniae Siciliae Sardiniae Latinae. Pars posterior. Inscriptiones Siciliae et Sardiniae, Corpus Inscriptionum Latinarum, consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae editum, 10.2 (Berlin: G. Reimer, 1883)
Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del museo civico di Termini Imerese
Livia Bivona, Iscrizioni latine lapidarie del museo civico di Termini Imerese, vol. 9/8, Kokalos Supplementi / Sikelika serie storica (Palermo / Rome, 1994)

ISic000104. Part of a monumental inscription

Text encoding: Tuuli Ahlholm, Robert Crellin

Text encoding: Tuuli Ahlholm, Robert Crellin

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text from autopsy ;
  • 1: The vertical stroke of a further letter is preserved on the break on the right hand edge, but given the lack of evidence from serifs, the possibilities are multiple; the last interpunct and this trace of a further letter both omitted by previous editors.

English translation

Tuuli Ahlholm

...son of Gnaeus, duumvir quinquennalis...

commentary

The absence of a cognomen should be noted. The slight traces of curvature along the edges suggests a circular structure?
